content:
Hello everyone,
I am currently focusing on the GPIO driver, specifically gpiolib.c and gpiolib-cdev.c. I find that gpio.rs has implemented the basic frame for the GPIO driver. I plan to rewrite certain functions and structs from gpiolib using Rust, such as struct gpio_device, struct gpio_desc, and gpiod_get_array_value, etc. Additionally, I intend to implement the basic character device interface to GPIO based on gpiolib-cdev.c using Rust.
Therefore, I want to ask if it is useful to do this work above?
Thanks!

If you mean useful for the kernel, i.e. if your goal is to upstream it to the kernel, then it will depend on the GPIO maintainers and the use case. The GPIO maintainers were interested in Rust in the past, so there is a chance they may still be. Please also see https://rust-for-linux.com/contributing#submitting-new-abstractions-and-modules.

Thanks, I have contacted the GPIO maintainers about the gpiolib issue, and I am aware that it is almost impossible to use rust to re-implement and maintain it. However, I find that "gpio_pl061_rust.c" implements the specific gpio controller driver using rust. And I want to rewrite the gpio controller driver of the rpi gpio as a try. I want to acquire if anyone is currently working on a similar project. I would appreciate your insights on this task.
Best regards!
I'm curious what the challenges of reimplementing it in rust are.

The gpio maintainer told that gpiolib is necessary for all the architectures that Linux supports, and some of these do not (yet) have a working Rust compiler, and there are more than 8000 LOC just for the library code, which is difficult to rewrite and maintain.
